project.title: 15 cm Aurora Gen3 column benchmarking on an Exploris 480
project.projectDescription: A comprehensive proteome map is essential to elucidate molecular pathways and protein functions. Although great improvements in sample preparation, instrumentation and data analysis already yield-ed impressive results, current studies suffer from a limited proteomic depth and dynamic range there-fore lacking low abundant or highly hydrophobic proteins. Here, we combine and benchmark advanced micro pillar array columns (µPAC) operated at nanoflow with Wide Window Acquisition (WWA) and the AI-based CHIMERYS search engine for data analysis to maximize chromatographic separation power, sensitivity and proteome coverage.  Our data shows that µPAC columns clearly outperform classical packed bed columns boosting peptide IDs by up to 50% and protein IDs by up to 24%. Using the above-mentioned analysis platform, more than 10,000 proteins could be identified from a single 2 h gradient shotgun analysis for a triple proteome mix of human, yeast and E. coli digests. At high sample loads of 400 ng all three uPAC types yielded comparable number of protein identifications, whereas the 50cm neo column performed best when lower inputs of less than 200 ng were injected. This additional dataset comprises additional data generated with the Aurora Elite G3 column (150 mm x 75 µm, 1.7 µm, IonOpticks) for comparison to the aforementioned µPAC technology.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Samples LC-MS/MS Peptide Reference Mix (V7491, Promega) spiked into human K562 cell digest (V6951, Promega). Samples were prepared in glass autosampler vials (Fisherbrand™ 9mm Short Thread TPX Vial with integrated glass micro-inserts, Cat. No. 11515924).  LC-MS/MS ANALYSIS 12.5 ng of the K562 cell digest with spiked in peptide reference mix was analyzed using a Vanquish Neo UHPLC with a trap-and-elute regime (for comparison of packed bed with µPAC columns). The LC was coupled to an Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer equipped with a FAIMS Pro interface (ThermoFisher Scientific). Analyte separation was performed using a classical packed bed column was used: Aurora Elite G3 (150 mm x 75 µm, 1.7 µm, IonOpticks, Australia) The column was operated at 50°C and connected to an EASY-Spray™ bullet emitter (10 µm ID, ES993; Thermo Fisher Scientific) An electrospray voltage of 2.3 kV was applied at the integrated liquid junction.  MS acquisition was performed in data-dependent mode, using a full scan with m/z range 380-1200, orbitrap resolution of 60.000, target value 100 %, and maximum injection time set to auto. -45V, -55V and -70V were used as FAIMS compensation voltages combined in a single run. The precursor intensity threshold was set to 1e4. Dynamic exclusion was set to 20s +-10ppm after the first isolation and fragmentation. Fragmentation by HCD was done using a normalized collision energy of 30 % and MS/MS spectra were acquired at a resolution of 15,000 with a precursor isolation width of 1 Th.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS spectra from raw data were imported to Proteome Discoverer (PD) (version 3.0.0.757, Thermo Scientific). Database search was performed using CHIMERYS as indicated against a human database  (uniprot reference, version 2022-03-04, 20,509 entries), as well as a common contaminant database (PD_Contaminants_IGGs_v17_tagsremoved, 344 entries). Trypsin was specified as proteolytic enzyme, cleaving after lysine (K) and arginine (R) except when followed by proline (P) and up to two missed cleavages were allowed. Oxidation of methionine (M) was set as variable modification. Identified spectra were rescored using Percolator and results were filtered for 1% FDR on peptide and protein level. Abundance of identified peptides was determined by label-free quantification (LFQ) using IMP-apQuant without match between runs (MBR).

references.referenceLine: Matzinger M, Schmücker A, Yelagandula R, Stejskal K, Krššáková G, Berger F, Mechtler K, Mayer RL. Micropillar arrays, wide window acquisition and AI-based data analysis improve comprehensiveness in multiple proteomic applications. Nat Commun. 2024 15(1):1019

Characteristics[Specimen]: QC4Life Reference standard (CS302403, Promega) that consists of LC-MS/MS Peptide Reference Mix (V7491, Promega) in a protein digest of K562 cells (V6951, Promega) diluted to 12.5 ng/5uL with 0.1% TFA
Characteristics[Organism]: protein digest of K562 cells (V6951, Promega)
Characteristics[MaterialType]: QC4Life Reference standard (CS302403, Promega) that consists of LC-MS/MS Peptide Reference Mix (V7491, Promega) in a protein digest of K562 cells (V6951, Promega) diluted to 12.5 ng/5uL with 0.1% TFA
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: Trypsin was specified as proteolytic enzyme, cleaving after lysine (K) and arginine I except when followed by proline (P) and up to two missed cleavages were allowed.
Comment[Instrument]: All samples were analyzed using a Vanquish Neo UHPLC operated in direct injection mode and coupled to the Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer equipped with a FAIMS Pro interface (ThermoFisher Scientific).
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: Fragmentation by HCD was done using a normalized collision energy of 30%
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: Fragment mass tolerance was limited to 20 ppm
Comment[GradientTime]: gradients ranging from 5 min to 120 min
Comment[IonizationType]: An electrospray voltage of 2.4 kV was applied at the integrated liquid junction of the EASY-Spray™ emitter
